blocks.content:
You are a performance engineer at a company that runs **quickpizza**, a pizza ordering API. Your team ran a spike test before a major launch. The test met every threshold—but your job is to decide whether the system is actually ready.

k6 gives you one view of the world. Prometheus probes, application logs, and traces give you complementary views. This path teaches you to chain those signals using a single investigation window.

## How this path is structured

1. Run the spike test in Grafana Cloud k6 and record a **test start time in UTC**. You reuse that window in Explore for metrics, logs, and traces.
1. Use Prometheus for regional, system-wide latency.
1. Use Loki for error class and ordering versus load.
1. Use Tempo for span-level latency and errors.
1. Write a short postmortem-style narrative and answer reflection prompts.

Keep a scratch pad for numbers (p95, peak VU minute, probe peaks, error text). You need them in the final milestone.
